name: Databricks coding-agent benchmark
task_surface: Recent human-written pull requests from a multi-million-line internal codebase
conclusion: Pi used about one-third as much context per turn. Some equal-quality matched runs differed by more than 2 times in cost.
limitation: The source does not publish task count, matched rows, run-level data or uncertainty.

name: Warden benchmark
task_surface: Known-vulnerability recognition in files tied to historical Sentry vulnerabilities
conclusion: Pi cost less and found a similar or larger share of the known corpus for the matched Claude models.
limitation: The comparator is Claude SDK, not the Claude Code command-line product. The benchmark does not measure whole-repository discovery.

name: scBench-Long
conclusion: Claude Code led Pi on Opus 4.8 while Codex and Pi tied on GPT-5.5. The reported intervals overlap widely.
limitation: The public summary does not expose cost. The confidence intervals are wide.

name: OpenBench M4.5
task_surface: Three repository coding tasks with executable checkers
conclusion: All clean harnesses passed every task. Pi and Cursor used less time and fewer tokens.
limitation: The tasks were too easy to separate frontier harnesses on correctness.

name: DrugDiscoveryBench
grader: Expert-authored rubrics scored by an LLM judge
data_access: Tasks are public; rubrics and reference answers require access approval
conclusion: GPT-5.5 performed best in mini-SWE-agent. Codex was neither the best nor worst harness for that model.
limitation: The paper plots cost but does not publish a table of exact cost points. The outcome depends on an LLM judge.

name: Coding Agent Index
task_surface: 321 tasks across DeepSWE, Terminal-Bench v2 and SWE-Atlas-QnA
grader: Program checks, test suites and rubric scoring
conclusion: OpenCode led the captured Opus 4.7 medium comparison. Claude Code was cheaper and faster.
limitation: The index averages 3 different benchmark families. The live results and harness versions can change.

name: Agent Security League
task_surface: Real vulnerability-fixing tasks with hidden functional and security tests
conclusion: Cursor led Claude Code for Opus 4.7 and 4.8. Claude Code led Cursor for Sonnet 5.
limitation: There are no repeated trials or task-level costs. Timeouts explain part of the reversal.

name: Vulnerability-Agent-Bench
task_surface: Real vulnerability repairs in isolated containers
conclusion: Claude Code and Codex led the corresponding OpenCode configurations on the published matched pairs.
limitation: The site alternates between 128 and 136 samples. Some reported cost differences need more explanation.

name: WildClawBench
task_surface: Bilingual, multimodal and long-horizon agent tasks
grader: Deterministic checks, environment checks and model judges
data_access: Code, tasks, images and evaluation details
conclusion: The best harness changed with the model. The source reports double-digit score changes from switching harness.
limitation: Each harness image has different tools and prompt conventions. Repeat counts are not clear in the headline table.

name: HarnessBench
task_surface: Recent bug-fix issues from 9 open-source repositories
conclusion: Several conditions finished within one task of the observed leader. Runtime differences were clearer than pass-rate differences.
limitation: The author states that the 27-task pass-rate differences are not statistically significant.

name: CORE-Bench Hard
conclusion: Claude Code more than doubled the verified success of CORE-Agent for Claude Opus 4.5 while costing less.
limitation: There is one run per configuration. The systems differ across retrieval, tools and context policy.

name: A-CODE-CLI
grader: Backend checks and Playwright user-interface checks
data_access: Public report and one example task
conclusion: OpenCode had a small backend lead over Claude Code and cost less in the matched Sonnet 4.6 comparison.
limitation: The benchmark has only 10 scenarios. Only one example task is public.

name: Harness Tax
task_surface: Two trivial prompts routed through a gateway
conclusion: The fixed input payload ranged from about 2,600 tokens for Pi to 27,000 for Claude Code.
limitation: This is an overhead diagnostic. It does not measure coding quality or full-session economics.
